actions: notify:Morning Briefing - A man has been found dead in his apartment from a knife wound. Victim identified as Daniel Ross, age 42, financial consultant. The crime scene and building are now available for investigation. Sarah Klein is available for questioning. Coroner's report received: preliminary autopsy results

notify:Day 2 Briefing - Lab results are arriving. Mark Bennett, Julia Ross, and Lucas Weber have been identified as additional persons of interest. Daniel's office is now accessible for investigation.
notify:Lab results are in - fingerprints from the crime scene have been identified.
notify:Lab results are in - the desk fingerprint has been identified as Mark Bennett's.
notify:Lab results are in - the hallway shoe print has been analyzed and matches women's size 38.
notify:The call log reveals a business connection. Daniel's office is now available for investigation.
notify:New lead: the accounting irregularities suggest there may be more hidden at Daniel's office.
notify:A key discovered in the safe may unlock something in the office desk.
notify:Warrant executed. Deleted messages from Julia's phone have been recovered.
notify:Search warrant executed at Julia's residence. Her shoes have been seized for comparison.
notify:Final day of investigation. You must submit your case report to the prosecutor before end of day.

interrogation_topics.dialogue: Daniel asked me to come by. It was supposed to be a quick conversation.
Like I said, around 20:30. Maybe a few minutes after, but not much later.

interrogation_topics.topic_name: What was your relationship with Daniel?
interrogation_topics.dialogue: We worked together for years. Things were tense lately, but that's business.

interrogation_topics.topic_name: Why did you lie about the time?
interrogation_topics.dialogue: Because it looks bad, alright? We argued. I didn't want to make myself look worse than I already do.

interrogation_topics.topic_name: What happened to the missing money?
interrogation_topics.dialogue: I made some transfers I shouldn't have. I was going to fix it before Daniel found out.
I don't know for sure... but Julia had been asking Daniel strange questions lately.
There was shouting. A man and... I don't know, maybe another voice. I couldn't make out every word.
I don't like getting involved in other people's problems. And it sounded serious. I just... stayed away.

interrogation_topics.topic_name: Did Daniel argue like this often?
interrogation_topics.dialogue: I heard raised voices before, sometimes. But not like that. That night felt different.

interrogation_topics.topic_name: Why didn't you say that earlier?
interrogation_topics.dialogue: I wasn't sure what I heard. And I didn't want to get in trouble for saying the wrong thing.
Quick steps. Like heels on the floor. Whoever it was didn't stop. They were gone in seconds.
It was dark. I only saw her for a second. She was... average height, maybe? Dark hair. I couldn't tell much more. I'm sorry.

interrogation_triggers.dialogue: Alright... maybe it was closer to 20:40. I didn't think ten minutes mattered.
Alright... maybe it was closer to 20:40. I didn't think ten minutes mattered.
This has nothing to do with Daniel's murder. You don't understand how messy business accounting can get.
This has nothing to do with Daniel's murder. You don't understand how messy business accounting can get.
Alright. That's everything. Daniel had the records. He was going to destroy me.
I... maybe I heard a woman's voice too. I wasn't sure. I didn't want to say something if I wasn't certain.
I heard footsteps outside my door. Fast ones. Like someone was trying not to stay there long.
I visited earlier in the day.
Okay... I stopped by briefly. But Daniel was alive when I left.
That... that doesn't prove anything! I told you I stopped by briefly!
He threatened to ruin everything. I just lost control.
Fine, I was working until 20:00, not 19:00. But I was in the basement the whole time, nowhere near his apartment.
See? My key card wasn't used for the elevator. I took the stairs to the basement. I never went to his floor.

interrogation_sessions.initial_dialogue:
I already told the police what happened.
I stopped by to talk to Daniel, we argued a little, and then I left.
That's it.
rejection_texts: That doesn't seem to shake his story.
That isn't enough to challenge this point.
interrogation_sessions.pressure_dialogue:
You're trying to pin this on me because I lied.
That doesn't make me a murderer.
interrogation_sessions.break_dialogue:
Fine.
Daniel found out about the money.
He said he was going to expose everything the next morning.
I panicked, alright?
But when I left, he was still alive.

interrogation_sessions.initial_dialogue:
I already told them what I know... I was in my apartment all evening.
I heard some arguing next door, but I didn't see anything.
rejection_texts: Sarah shakes her head. 'I don't know anything about that.'
She looks confused. 'I... I don't understand what you're showing me.'
She wrings her hands. 'That doesn't mean anything to me.'
She avoids your eyes. 'I really can't help you with that.'
interrogation_sessions.pressure_dialogue: Sarah fidgets. 'Please... I just want to stay out of this. I'm scared enough already.'
interrogation_sessions.break_dialogue:
I was scared, alright?
I did look. Just for a second.
I saw a woman leaving Daniel's apartment.
She was moving fast, like she didn't want anyone to see her.
I didn't say anything because I didn't want to be dragged into this.

interrogation_sessions.initial_dialogue: Julia sits rigid, arms crossed. 'Daniel was my husband. We were separated, but I had nothing to do with this.'
rejection_texts: She waves dismissively. 'This has nothing to do with me.'
'You're wasting both of our time with this.'

interrogation_sessions.initial_dialogue: Lucas leans back casually. 'I'm the building maintenance guy. I was in the basement working all evening.'
rejection_texts: Lucas shrugs. 'Don't know what that has to do with me.'
He looks bored. 'I was in the basement. That's my story.'
interrogation_sessions.pressure_dialogue: Lucas shifts uncomfortably. 'Look, I just fix pipes. I don't get involved in tenants' business.'
interrogation_sessions.break_dialogue:
Lucas sighs heavily.
'Alright, fine. I wasn't in the basement the whole time.
I came up to grab a tool from the supply closet on the third floor around 20:45.
I heard yelling from the apartment — a man and a woman going at it.
I didn't see anything, but the voice... it wasn't the neighbor lady.'
